rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA MILLS VS. BOWLERO CORPORATION, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, September 29, 2025, Line 2. CROSS DEFENDANTs VENTURA INTERMEDIATE INC., VENTURA BLVD HOLDINGS INC., VENTURA BLVD SUPER HOLDINGS INC.,and VENTURA BLVD. ENTERTAINMENT, L.L.C.'s MOTION TO DISMISS. Cross-Defendants Ventura Blvd. Entertainment. LLC, Ventura Blvd Holdings, Inc., Ventura Blvd Super Holdings Inc. and Ventura Intermediate Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss, or in the Alternative to Stay, Cross-Complaint for Forum Non Conveniens is GRANTED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, September 29, 2025, Line 10. PLAINTIFF ALEXIA HAIRE's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Before the court is a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel from Counsel John P. Strouss III and Law Offices of John P. Strouss III, counsel of record for Plaintiff Alexia Rain Haire. HEARING REQUIRED. Prior to the hearing, Moving Parties shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a completed proposed order on MC-053 that includes accurate information regarding next court date and, if any, trial date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
